author: Benedikt Schnatterbeck
text: Query scoping: by default, queries should be scoped to the current project's brain (resolved from .brain/brain.toml marker in cwd). Add a --global or --all flag to search across all registered brains. The central registry at ~/.brain/config.toml already knows all brains, so the query command can resolve which LanceDB/SQLite stores to hit. This enables cross-project search while keeping the default behavior project-local.

author: Benedikt Schnatterbeck
text: Code review finding: scan_brain() in scanner.rs performs synchronous blocking I/O (WalkDir + std::fs::read_to_string) and is called from the async full_scan() in pipeline.rs:219. For large note directories this blocks the tokio runtime thread, starving watcher event processing. When implementing the concurrency model, ensure scan_brain is wrapped in spawn_blocking, or restructured to run on its own thread.

author: Benedikt Schnatterbeck
text: Code review finding: chunker.rs:84-94 split_long_text() slices by byte offset (remaining[..max_len]) which will panic on multi-byte UTF-8 characters (emoji, CJK, accented chars) if the boundary lands mid-character. This should be caught by the byte-offset correctness tests. Consider using floor_char_boundary() or manual char boundary detection.

author: Benedikt Schnatterbeck
text: Implementation note: avoid double-fork daemon pattern. launchd and systemd both expect to manage the process directly — a double-forked daemon fights the service manager. Instead: (1) brain daemon start should spawn 'brain watch' as a detached child via Command::new().spawn(), write PID to .brain/brain.pid, redirect stdout/stderr to .brain/brain.log. (2) brain daemon install (in brain-315) generates a launchd/systemd unit that runs 'brain watch' directly — no daemon wrapper needed. The daemon start subcommand is a convenience for users who don't want to set up a system service; the OS service is the primary path.

author: Benedikt Schnatterbeck
text: QA review findings addressed: (1) tightened loose assertions — heading_splits now exact 6, fixture_headings exact 21, fixture indexed count exact 9; (2) score assertions no longer silently skip — added explicit is_some() guard before unwrap; (3) added test_chunker_fixture_large validating all chunks <= MAX_CHUNK_CHARS; (4) documented semantic relevance limitation in module doc — MockEmbedder validates plumbing via identity matching, not true semantic similarity. Golden DB approach deferred to CI setup.

author: Benedikt Schnatterbeck
text: Note from smoke test QA review (brain-0de.10): when CI is set up, consider adding a golden test database built with real BGE-small model weights for semantic relevance testing. The current smoke tests use MockEmbedder (hash-based) which validates pipeline plumbing but not semantic query quality. A golden DB generated from tests/fixtures/ could be cached as a CI artifact and used by query-only tests that verify e.g. 'How to cook pasta?' returns cooking.md above gardening.md.
